exemplar.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Consistent focus on George Boxâs aphorism with clear links to both mathematics and the natural sciences
Use of specific, real-world and theoretical examples (e.g. wave-particle duality, Taylor series, Euclidean vs non-Euclidean geometry) that illustrate the trade-off between accuracy and utility
Awareness of contrasting perspectives, acknowledging how some âwrongâ models can still yield practical benefits
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely explanatory with only surface-level evaluation of underlying assumptions and implications
Several conceptual imprecisions (for example mischaracterizing the scope of general relativity) that weaken the precision of the argument
Inadequate integration of TOK conventions, including the absence of an explicit knowledge question and underdeveloped citation of sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the essay exhibits strong command of examples and maintains a clear, sustained focus on the prescribed titleâdemonstrating effective linkage between the prescribed title and two areas of knowledgeâthe analytical depth and precision necessary for the very highest band are not fully realized. The studentâs use of real-world and theoretical illustrations, alongside an emerging engagement with alternative viewpoints, elevates the discussion well into the upper bands. However, recurring conceptual inaccuracies, a lack of a clearly stated knowledge question, and only surface-level evaluation of implications and assumptions constrain the work from achieving a perfect mark. On balance, allocating 9 marks reflects its overall high quality, while recognizing the areas that fall just short of the criteria for the topmost band.

annotations.comment: The table simplifies complex quantum phenomena into binary classifications, which risks misleading the reader. A more nuanced discussion of how the wavefunction formalism unifies these models would deepen analysis.
The essay articulates how complementarity in quantum mechanics yields practical technologies, demonstrating the beneficial interplay between two âwrongâ models to produce new tools.
The reference to complementarity would benefit from acknowledgement of Niels Bohrâs principle. Citing key theorists would add depth to the historical and conceptual context.
This summarising sentence is overly generic and interrupts the flow. A more concise recap would improve cohesion before introducing the next mathematical example.
The in-text citation â(LibreTexts Chemistry)â is not fully integrated. The student should adopt TOK conventions for references and explain why this source strengthens the argument in context.

The introduction articulates the notion of a modelâs purpose but fails to formulate an explicit knowledge question. The student should clearly state a focused TOK question (e.g., âTo what extent do Boxâs criteria of usefulness and truth affect the production of knowledge in different AOKs?â) to guide the essay.
The paragraph effectively defines the tension between accuracy and utility in models, showing clear understanding of how simplification facilitates prediction in both science and mathematics.
Use of wave-particle duality as an example demonstrates relevant engagement with quantum mechanics and connects well to Boxâs claim about model utility despite inaccuracy.
There is no critical evaluation of why these particular phenomena were chosen. The student should justify selection criteria and discuss why some cases challenge Boxâs dichotomy of right vs useful.
The introduction omits any explicit reference to ways of knowing or knowledge framework. Integrating terms like âreasonâ or âlanguageâ would enrich the TOK perspective on model construction.
The student should include a concrete example (e.g., approximation of $$e^x$$ or gravitational potential) to illustrate how the Taylor modelâs utility varies with the number of terms.
Contrasting Euclidean and non-Euclidean geometries demonstrates insightful awareness of how foundational axioms influence a modelâs applicability in real-world contexts.
Describing the parallel postulate as âincorrectâ oversimplifies the status of axioms. The student should explain that axioms are assumptions within a system, not empirically true or false.

The suggestion that âincorrect models inspire new researchâ is intriguing but underdeveloped. The essay should examine whether this outcome qualifies as âusefulâ under Boxâs definition or represents a separate epistemic value.
The conclusion effectively recognises that model validity evolves with new evidence, reflecting a nuanced appreciation of the dynamic nature of knowledge and utility.
The student could deepen the analysis by linking this discussion explicitly to the knowledge question, showing how complementary models influence our understanding of âtruthâ in science.

The claim that general relativity âdoesnât account for high-speed motionâ is inaccurate. Special relativity addresses constant high-speed motion, while GR extends to gravity; this conceptual slip undermines the argumentâs precision.

There is a missed opportunity to connect the limitations of GR back to Boxâs claim: the student should analyse how context-dependent breakdowns of a model inform its utility in knowledge production.
The transition from physics to mathematics is abrupt. A clearer linking sentence would help maintain coherence and remind the reader of the central knowledge question.
The explanation of Taylor Series and the radius of convergence is thorough and shows strong grasp of how mathematical approximations balance complexity and accuracy.
There is no discussion of how the choice of expansion point affects model validity. Evaluating the impact of centre selection would deepen the critical reflection on model scope.
Figure 2 is presented without discussion in the main text. The student needs to analyse how this diagram supports the argument about parallel lines to strengthen the link to Boxâs claim.

The conclusion remains largely descriptive. It would benefit from critical evaluation of the implications for knowledge production, such as how model shifts impact scientific consensus and methodology.
There is no revisiting of the initial knowledge question or explicit framing of arguments around it in the conclusion. The student should restate and directly answer the question to achieve closure.

The essay focuses on mathematics and natural sciences exclusively. For stronger evaluation of Boxâs claim, the student could briefly consider a contrasting AOK (e.g., human sciences) to broaden the scope of analysis.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
